comments.body: Usually, for maven/gradle builds, in the bottom pannel, see left side. There you shall see a hierarchy of logs and if you select the root level one, you shall see the exact arguments that were used by intellij to build. By default, it shows you the last (leaf node in that hierarchy) so you see only end result/short data. Hope it was useful. Let me know if you have difficulty in finding this or I misinterpreted your concern.

answers.body:
What you can do is go to

File -> settings -> Build, Execution, Deployment -> Build Tools -> Maven. There you can configure Output level to debug which would make maven build output most available log information.

comments.body: As it’s currently written, your answer is unclear. Please edit to add additional details that will help others understand how this addresses the question asked. You can find more information on how to write good answers in the help center.

answers.body:
To view the output of an maven build: You should use the "m"-Button on right side. In the appearing maven-Toolbar press the "Execute Maven Goal"-Button and doubleclick the "mvn install"-goal. The maven output will be printed on the left side in "run"-output panel. This is not the output on the usual build panel.

Screenshot

title: How to see Full Build output of a java/mvn project in IntelliJ
body:
I am a using intelliJ IDE community edition for a Java project with mvn.pom. I build the project using Ctrl+F9 key. The Build tab in intelliJ only shows a very short/summarized output.

I want to see details of actual build process for example what arguments are being passed to javac etc as a result of the build etc. Any help would be appreciated. thanks
